title: Mimi's Narrow Hall Tree Hutch
summary: A narrow standalone cabinet with a shelf and divider behind a beadboard back, capped with a small crown moulding detail — a compact hutch, not a bench-with-hooks hall tree.
description:
This is a genuine cabinet, unlike this catalog's other hall tree pieces, which pair a bench seat with a hook rail — here it's just two sides, a shelf, and a divider, forming a small open case sized to slip into a narrow entryway. Build the case first, then jigsaw-fit the beadboard or 1/4" plywood back across the whole opening.

Front and back trim pieces frame the case opening, with the front trim proud enough to give the piece a finished edge, and a solid 1x6 base under the whole assembly to keep it stable. **The 1x12 top overhangs the case** and gets a small crown moulding detail applied beneath it — this cap-and-crown combination is what elevates the piece from a plain shelf to a genuine hutch.

Use either 2" screws or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws throughout, whichever matches the tools already on hand.

tools.note: For fitting the beadboard or plywood back panel.

materials.name: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the case
steps.body: Join the two sides with the shelf and divider to form the open case.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Add the trim and base
steps.body: Attach the front and back trim around the case opening, then attach the base underneath.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Fit the back panel
steps.body: Jigsaw-fit the beadboard or plywood back panel to the case opening and nail in place.

steps.title: Add the top and crown moulding
steps.body: Attach the overhanging top, then miter and apply the small crown moulding beneath it.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Narrow hall tree hutch cabinet with crown moulding top
